rules.name: disable-enable-pair
rules.description: require a `eslint-enable` comment for every `eslint-disable` comment

rules.name: no-aggregating-enable
rules.description: disallow a `eslint-enable` comment for multiple `eslint-disable` comments

rules.name: no-duplicate-disable
rules.description: disallow duplicate `eslint-disable` comments

rules.name: no-restricted-disable
rules.description: disallow `eslint-disable` comments about specific rules

rules.name: no-unlimited-disable
rules.description: disallow `eslint-disable` comments without rule names

rules.name: no-unused-disable
rules.description: disallow unused `eslint-disable` comments

rules.name: no-unused-enable
rules.description: disallow unused `eslint-enable` comments

rules.name: no-use
rules.description: disallow ESLint directive-comments

rules.name: require-description
rules.description: require include descriptions in ESLint directive-comments

rules.name: functional-parameters
rules.description: Enforce functional parameters.

rules.name: immutable-data
rules.description: Enforce treating data as immutable.

rules.name: no-class-inheritance
rules.description: Disallow inheritance in classes.

rules.name: no-classes
rules.description: Disallow classes.

rules.name: no-conditional-statements
rules.description: Disallow conditional statements.

rules.name: no-expression-statements
rules.description: Disallow expression statements.

rules.name: no-let
rules.description: Disallow mutable variables.

rules.name: no-loop-statements
rules.description: Disallow imperative loops.

rules.name: no-mixed-types
rules.description: Restrict types so that only members of the same kind are allowed in them.

rules.name: no-promise-reject
rules.description: Disallow rejecting promises.

rules.name: no-return-void
rules.description: Disallow functions that don't return anything.

rules.name: no-this-expressions
rules.description: Disallow this access.

rules.name: no-throw-statements
rules.description: Disallow throwing exceptions.

rules.name: no-try-statements
rules.description: Disallow try-catch[-finally] and try-finally patterns.

rules.name: prefer-immutable-types
rules.description: Require function parameters to be typed as certain immutability

rules.name: prefer-property-signatures
rules.description: Prefer property signatures over method signatures.

rules.name: prefer-readonly-type
rules.description: Prefer readonly types over mutable types.

rules.name: prefer-tacit
rules.description: Replaces `x => f(x)` with just `f`.

rules.name: readonly-type
rules.description: Require consistently using either `readonly` keywords or `Readonly<T>`

rules.name: type-declaration-immutability
rules.description: Enforce the immutability of types based on patterns.

rules.name: confusing-quantifier
rules.description: disallow confusing quantifiers

rules.name: control-character-escape
rules.description: enforce consistent escaping of control characters

rules.name: grapheme-string-literal
rules.description: enforce single grapheme in string literal

rules.name: hexadecimal-escape
rules.description: enforce consistent usage of hexadecimal escape

rules.name: letter-case
rules.description: enforce into your favorite case

rules.name: match-any
rules.description: enforce match any character style

rules.name: negation
rules.description: enforce use of escapes on negation

rules.name: no-contradiction-with-assertion
rules.description: disallow elements that contradict assertions

rules.name: no-control-character
rules.description: disallow control characters

rules.name: no-dupe-characters-character-class
rules.description: disallow duplicate characters in the RegExp character class

rules.name: no-dupe-disjunctions
rules.description: disallow duplicate disjunctions

rules.name: no-empty-alternative
rules.description: disallow alternatives without elements

rules.name: no-empty-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow capturing group that captures empty.

rules.name: no-empty-character-class
rules.description: disallow character classes that match no characters

rules.name: no-empty-group
rules.description: disallow empty group

rules.name: no-empty-lookarounds-assertion
rules.description: disallow empty lookahead assertion or empty lookbehind assertion

rules.name: no-empty-string-literal
rules.description: disallow empty string literals in character classes

rules.name: no-escape-backspace
rules.description: disallow escape backspace (`[\\b]`)

rules.name: no-extra-lookaround-assertions
rules.description: disallow unnecessary nested lookaround assertions

rules.name: no-invalid-regexp
rules.description: disallow invalid regular expression strings in `RegExp` constructors

rules.name: no-invisible-character
rules.description: disallow invisible raw character

rules.name: no-lazy-ends
rules.description: disallow lazy quantifiers at the end of an expression

rules.name: no-legacy-features
rules.description: disallow legacy RegExp features

rules.name: no-misleading-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow capturing groups that do not behave as one would expect

rules.name: no-misleading-unicode-character
rules.description: disallow multi-code-point characters in character classes and quantifiers

rules.name: no-missing-g-flag
rules.description: disallow missing `g` flag in patterns used in `String#matchAll` and `String#replaceAll`

rules.name: no-non-standard-flag
rules.description: disallow non-standard flags

rules.name: no-obscure-range
rules.description: disallow obscure character ranges

rules.name: no-octal
rules.description: disallow octal escape sequence

rules.name: no-optional-assertion
rules.description: disallow optional assertions

rules.name: no-potentially-useless-backreference
rules.description: disallow backreferences that reference a group that might not be matched

rules.name: no-standalone-backslash
rules.description: disallow standalone backslashes (`\\`)

rules.name: no-super-linear-backtracking
rules.description: disallow exponential and polynomial backtracking

rules.name: no-super-linear-move
rules.description: disallow quantifiers that cause quadratic moves

rules.name: no-trivially-nested-assertion
rules.description: disallow trivially nested assertions

rules.name: no-trivially-nested-quantifier
rules.description: disallow nested quantifiers that can be rewritten as one quantifier

rules.name: no-unused-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow unused capturing group

rules.name: no-useless-assertions
rules.description: disallow assertions that are known to always accept (or reject)

rules.name: no-useless-backreference
rules.description: disallow useless backreferences in regular expressions

rules.name: no-useless-character-class
rules.description: disallow character class with one character

rules.name: no-useless-dollar-replacements
rules.description: disallow useless `$` replacements in replacement string

rules.name: no-useless-escape
rules.description: disallow unnecessary escape characters in RegExp

rules.name: no-useless-flag
rules.description: disallow unnecessary regex flags

rules.name: no-useless-lazy
rules.description: disallow unnecessarily non-greedy quantifiers

rules.name: no-useless-non-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow unnecessary non-capturing group

rules.name: no-useless-quantifier
rules.description: disallow quantifiers that can be removed

rules.name: no-useless-range
rules.description: disallow unnecessary character ranges

rules.name: no-useless-set-operand
rules.description: disallow unnecessary elements in expression character classes

rules.name: no-useless-string-literal
rules.description: disallow string disjunction of single characters in `\\q{...}`

rules.name: no-useless-two-nums-quantifier
rules.description: disallow unnecessary `{n,m}` quantifier

rules.name: no-zero-quantifier
rules.description: disallow quantifiers with a maximum of zero

rules.name: optimal-lookaround-quantifier
rules.description: disallow the alternatives of lookarounds that end with a non-constant quantifier

rules.name: optimal-quantifier-concatenation
rules.description: require optimal quantifiers for concatenated quantifiers

rules.name: prefer-character-class
rules.description: enforce using character class

rules.name: prefer-d
rules.description: enforce using `\\d`

rules.name: prefer-escape-replacement-dollar-char
rules.description: enforces escape of replacement `$` character (`$$`).

rules.name: prefer-lookaround
rules.description: prefer lookarounds over capturing group that do not replace

rules.name: prefer-named-backreference
rules.description: enforce using named backreferences

rules.name: prefer-named-capture-group
rules.description: enforce using named capture groups

rules.name: prefer-named-replacement
rules.description: enforce using named replacement

rules.name: prefer-plus-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using `+` quantifier

rules.name: prefer-predefined-assertion
rules.description: prefer predefined assertion over equivalent lookarounds

rules.name: prefer-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using quantifier

rules.name: prefer-question-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using `?` quantifier

rules.name: prefer-range
rules.description: enforce using character class range

rules.name: prefer-regexp-exec
rules.description: enforce that `RegExp#exec` is used instead of `String#match` if no global flag is provided

rules.name: prefer-regexp-test
rules.description: enforce that `RegExp#test` is used instead of `String#match` and `RegExp#exec`

rules.name: prefer-result-array-groups
rules.description: enforce using result array `groups`

rules.name: prefer-set-operation
rules.description: prefer character class set operations instead of lookarounds

rules.name: prefer-star-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using `*` quantifier

rules.name: prefer-unicode-codepoint-escapes
rules.description: enforce use of unicode codepoint escapes

rules.name: prefer-w
rules.description: enforce using `\\w`

rules.name: require-unicode-regexp
rules.description: enforce the use of the `u` flag

rules.name: require-unicode-sets-regexp
rules.description: enforce the use of the `v` flag

rules.name: simplify-set-operations
rules.description: require simplify set operations

rules.name: sort-alternatives
rules.description: sort alternatives if order doesn't matter

rules.name: sort-character-class-elements
rules.description: enforces elements order in character class

rules.name: sort-flags
rules.description: require regex flags to be sorted

rules.name: strict
rules.description: disallow not strictly valid regular expressions

rules.name: unicode-escape
rules.description: enforce consistent usage of unicode escape or unicode codepoint escape

rules.name: unicode-property
rules.description: enforce consistent naming of unicode properties

rules.name: use-ignore-case
rules.description: use the `i` flag if it simplifies the pattern

rules.name: detect-bidi-characters
rules.description: Detects trojan source attacks that employ unicode bidi attacks to inject malicious code.

rules.name: detect-buffer-noassert
rules.description: Detects calls to "buffer" with "noAssert" flag set.

rules.name: detect-child-process
rules.description: Detects instances of "child_process" & non-literal "exec()" calls.

rules.name: detect-disable-mustache-escape
rules.description: Detects "object.escapeMarkup = false", which can be used with some template engines to disable escaping of HTML entities.

rules.name: detect-eval-with-expression
rules.description: Detects "eval(variable)" which can allow an attacker to run arbitrary code inside your process.

rules.name: detect-new-buffer
rules.description: Detects instances of new Buffer(argument) where argument is any non-literal value.

rules.name: detect-no-csrf-before-method-override
rules.description: Detects Express "csrf" middleware setup before "method-override" middleware.

rules.name: detect-non-literal-fs-filename
rules.description: Detects variable in filename argument of "fs" calls, which might allow an attacker to access anything on your system.

rules.name: detect-non-literal-regexp
rules.description: Detects "RegExp(variable)", which might allow an attacker to DOS your server with a long-running regular expression.

rules.name: detect-non-literal-require
rules.description: Detects "require(variable)", which might allow an attacker to load and run arbitrary code, or access arbitrary files on disk.

rules.name: detect-object-injection
rules.description: Detects "variable[key]" as a left- or right-hand assignment operand.

rules.name: detect-possible-timing-attacks
rules.description: Detects insecure comparisons (`==`, `!=`, `!==` and `===`), which check input sequentially.

rules.name: detect-pseudoRandomBytes
rules.description: Detects if "pseudoRandomBytes()" is in use, which might not give you the randomness you need and expect.

rules.name: detect-unsafe-regex
rules.description: Detects potentially unsafe regular expressions, which may take a very long time to run, blocking the event loop.

rules.name: exports
rules.description: Automatically sort exports.

rules.name: imports
rules.description: Automatically sort imports.

rules.name: await-interactions
rules.description: Interactions should be awaited

rules.name: context-in-play-function
rules.description: Pass a context when invoking play function of another story

rules.name: csf-component
rules.description: The component property should be set

rules.name: default-exports
rules.description: Story files should have a default export

rules.name: hierarchy-separator
rules.description: Deprecated hierarchy separator in title property

rules.name: meta-inline-properties
rules.description: Meta should only have inline properties

rules.name: meta-satisfies-type
rules.description: Meta should use `satisfies Meta`

rules.name: no-redundant-story-name
rules.description: A story should not have a redundant name property

rules.name: no-renderer-packages
rules.description: Do not import renderer packages directly in stories

rules.name: no-stories-of
rules.description: storiesOf is deprecated and should not be used

rules.name: no-title-property-in-meta
rules.description: Do not define a title in meta

rules.name: no-uninstalled-addons
rules.description: This rule identifies storybook addons that are invalid because they are either not installed or contain a typo in their name.

rules.name: prefer-pascal-case
rules.description: Stories should use PascalCase

rules.name: story-exports
rules.description: A story file must contain at least one story export

rules.name: use-storybook-expect
rules.description: Use expect from `@storybook/test`, `storybook/test` or `@storybook/jest`

rules.name: use-storybook-testing-library
rules.description: Do not use testing-library directly on stories

rules.name: blocklist
rules.description: Utilities in UnoCSS blocklist

rules.name: enforce-class-compile
rules.description: Enforce class compilation

rules.name: order
rules.description: Order of UnoCSS utilities in class attribute

rules.name: order-attributify
rules.description: Order of UnoCSS attributes

rules.name: no-duplicate-keys
rules.description: Disallow duplicate keys in JSON objects

rules.name: no-empty-keys
rules.description: Disallow empty keys in JSON objects

rules.name: no-unnormalized-keys
rules.description: Disallow JSON keys that are not normalized

rules.name: no-unsafe-values
rules.description: Disallow JSON values that are unsafe for interchange

rules.name: sort-keys
rules.description: Require JSON object keys to be sorted

rules.name: top-level-interop
rules.description: Require the JSON top-level value to be an array or object

rules.name: fenced-code-language
rules.description: Require languages for fenced code blocks

rules.name: fenced-code-meta
rules.description: Require or disallow metadata for fenced code blocks

rules.name: heading-increment
rules.description: Enforce heading levels increment by one

rules.name: no-bare-urls
rules.description: Disallow bare URLs

rules.name: no-duplicate-definitions
rules.description: Disallow duplicate definitions

rules.name: no-duplicate-headings
rules.description: Disallow duplicate headings in the same document

rules.name: no-empty-definitions
rules.description: Disallow empty definitions

rules.name: no-empty-images
rules.description: Disallow empty images

rules.name: no-empty-links
rules.description: Disallow empty links

rules.name: no-html
rules.description: Disallow HTML tags

rules.name: no-invalid-label-refs
rules.description: Disallow invalid label references

rules.name: no-missing-atx-heading-space
rules.description: Disallow headings without a space after the hash characters

rules.name: no-missing-label-refs
rules.description: Disallow missing label references

rules.name: no-missing-link-fragments
rules.description: Disallow link fragments that do not reference valid headings

rules.name: no-multiple-h1
rules.description: Disallow multiple H1 headings in the same document

rules.name: no-reference-like-urls
rules.description: Disallow URLs that match defined reference identifiers

rules.name: no-reversed-media-syntax
rules.description: Disallow reversed link and image syntax

rules.name: no-space-in-emphasis
rules.description: Disallow spaces around emphasis markers

rules.name: no-unused-definitions
rules.description: Disallow unused definitions

rules.name: require-alt-text
rules.description: Require alternative text for images

rules.name: table-column-count
rules.description: Disallow data rows in a GitHub Flavored Markdown table from having more cells than the header row

rules.name: align-column-definitions
rules.description: Align column definitions vertically inside `CREATE TABLE` so that name, type, and constraints share consistent column offsets

rules.name: align-values
rules.description: Align column values vertically inside multi-row `INSERT ... VALUES (...)` so that each tuple position shares a consistent width across rows

rules.name: consistent-as-for-column-alias
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the `AS` keyword before column aliases in `SELECT` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-as-for-table-alias
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the `AS` keyword before table aliases (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-between-over-and
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `x BETWEEN a AND b` vs `x >= a AND x <= b` for closed-interval range checks

rules.name: consistent-create-index-concurrently
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `CREATE INDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-create-or-replace
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CREATE OR REPLACE` for `FUNCTION` / `PROCEDURE` / `VIEW` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-drop-index-concurrently
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `DROP INDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-explicit-inner-join
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the explicit `INNER` keyword in `INNER JOIN` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-explicit-outer-join
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the explicit `OUTER` keyword in `LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOIN` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-fk-not-valid
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `NOT VALID` for `ALTER TABLE ... ADD FOREIGN KEY` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-identity-over-serial
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY` vs `SERIAL` / `BIGSERIAL` / `SMALLSERIAL`

rules.name: consistent-jsonb-over-json
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `jsonb` vs `json` for column types

rules.name: consistent-reindex-concurrently
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `REINDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-text-over-varchar
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `text` vs `varchar(n)` for string columns

rules.name: consistent-timestamptz
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `timestamptz` vs `timestamp` (without time zone)

rules.name: no-add-check-constraint-without-not-valid
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... CHECK (...)` without `NOT VALID`; the synchronous form holds `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` on the table for the entire validating scan

rules.name: no-add-column-not-null-without-default
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NOT NULL` without a `DEFAULT` because the migration fails outright on any non-empty table

rules.name: no-add-unique-constraint-directly
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE (...)` written inline; build the index with `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` first, then promote it via `ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE USING INDEX <name>`

rules.name: no-alter-column-type
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE ...` because it can rewrite the table under an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock

rules.name: no-char-type
rules.description: Disallow the blank-padded `char(n)` / `bpchar` column type

rules.name: no-cluster
rules.description: Disallow the `CLUSTER` statement: it takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE, rewrites the table, and is not maintained afterwards

rules.name: no-composite-primary-key
rules.description: Disallow composite (multi-column) PRIMARY KEY constraints; use a single surrogate key and a UNIQUE constraint on the natural columns instead

rules.name: no-create-role
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE ROLE` / `CREATE USER` in application migrations; manage roles in a separate operator workflow

rules.name: no-cross-join
rules.description: Disallow `CROSS JOIN` (unqualified cartesian product)

rules.name: no-distinct-on-without-order-by
rules.description: Disallow `SELECT DISTINCT ON (...)` without an `ORDER BY`; the surviving row in each group is otherwise non-deterministic

rules.name: no-drop-column
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN` — every reader of the dropped column breaks at deploy time

rules.name: no-drop-database
rules.description: Disallow `DROP DATABASE`; it is catastrophic if run by accident and should not live in versioned SQL

rules.name: no-drop-not-null
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER COLUMN ... DROP NOT NULL` — relaxing a NOT NULL constraint surprises every consumer that already assumes the column is non-null

rules.name: no-drop-schema-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `DROP SCHEMA ... CASCADE`; it silently removes every object in the schema

rules.name: no-drop-table-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `DROP TABLE ... CASCADE` because it silently removes dependent objects

rules.name: no-equality-with-null
rules.description: Disallow `x = NULL` / `x <> NULL`; PostgreSQL's three-valued logic makes both expressions evaluate to NULL (i.e. neither true nor false), which silently filters away rows the author probably wanted

rules.name: no-grant-all
rules.description: Disallow `GRANT ALL` / `GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES`; enumerate the privileges actually needed so the grant is auditable and a future PG release adding a new privilege does not silently extend it

rules.name: no-grant-to-public
rules.description: Disallow GRANT statements that target the `PUBLIC` pseudo-role

rules.name: no-group-by-ordinal
rules.description: Disallow `GROUP BY <position>` (positional/ordinal references); use the column name or expression instead

rules.name: no-having-without-group-by
rules.description: Disallow `HAVING` without `GROUP BY` — the query aggregates the entire result set, which is almost never the intended shape

rules.name: no-identifier-too-long
rules.description: Disallow identifiers longer than PostgreSQL's `NAMEDATALEN - 1` limit (default 63 bytes)

rules.name: no-implicit-join
rules.description: Disallow comma-separated FROM clauses (implicit cross joins); use explicit JOIN syntax

rules.name: no-leading-wildcard-like
rules.description: Disallow `LIKE`/`ILIKE` patterns that begin with `%` because they cannot use a B-tree index and force a full scan

rules.name: no-money-type
rules.description: Disallow the `money` column type

rules.name: no-natural-join
rules.description: Disallow `NATURAL JOIN`

rules.name: no-not-in-subquery
rules.description: Disallow `NOT IN (subquery)` because NULL values in the subquery silently return zero rows

rules.name: no-numeric-without-precision
rules.description: Require an explicit precision (and scale) on `NUMERIC` / `DECIMAL` columns

rules.name: no-on-delete-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `ON DELETE CASCADE` on foreign keys; cascading deletes are easy to write but can wipe out far more rows than the author intended

rules.name: no-order-by-ordinal
rules.description: Disallow `ORDER BY <position>` (positional/ordinal references); use the column name or alias instead

rules.name: no-rename-column
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN` — every deployed reader of the old name breaks at deploy time

rules.name: no-rename-table
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO` — every deployed reader of the old name breaks at deploy time

rules.name: no-rule
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE RULE`; PostgreSQL's rule system is a known foot-gun and is effectively deprecated in favor of triggers and views

rules.name: no-security-definer-without-search-path
rules.description: Disallow `SECURITY DEFINER` functions that do not pin `search_path`; an attacker who controls a schema in the caller's `search_path` can shadow built-in objects and escalate privileges

rules.name: no-select-into
rules.description: Disallow `SELECT ... INTO target FROM ...` (creates a new table); use `CREATE TABLE AS SELECT ...` instead

rules.name: no-select-star
rules.description: Disallow `SELECT *` in queries to keep result schemas stable when the underlying table changes

rules.name: no-set-not-null
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER COLUMN ... SET NOT NULL` because it scans the whole table under ACCESS EXCLUSIVE

rules.name: no-set-search-path
rules.description: Disallow `SET search_path = ...` in versioned SQL; qualify identifiers with their schema instead

rules.name: no-syntax-error
rules.description: Disallow PostgreSQL syntax errors

rules.name: no-temporary-table
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` in versioned SQL — temp tables exist for the session only and rarely belong in migration files

rules.name: no-time-type
rules.description: Disallow `TIME` / `TIME WITH TIME ZONE` columns; they rarely model a real-world value correctly

rules.name: no-truncate-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `TRUNCATE ... CASCADE` because it transitively empties referencing tables

rules.name: no-unlogged-table
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE` because unlogged tables are truncated on crash and not replicated

rules.name: no-unnecessary-quoted-identifier
rules.description: Disallow unnecessary double-quoting of identifiers (e.g. `"users"` when `users` would mean the same thing)

rules.name: no-update-primary-key
rules.description: Disallow `UPDATE ... SET <pk> = ...` for columns the rule treats as primary keys

rules.name: no-update-without-from-binding
rules.description: Disallow `UPDATE ... FROM other_table` without a `WHERE` clause; without a join condition the FROM table forms a Cartesian product with the target table and updates every row of the target as many times as `other_table` has rows

rules.name: no-vacuum-full
rules.description: Disallow `VACUUM FULL` because it takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE and rewrites the entire table

rules.name: no-volatile-default-on-add-column
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT <volatile>()`; volatile defaults force a full table rewrite under `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` because the stable-default short-cut cannot be used

rules.name: no-with-recursive-without-limit
rules.description: Disallow `WITH RECURSIVE` queries that have no `LIMIT` on the outer SELECT, which can run unboundedly if the recursion's termination condition is wrong

rules.name: plpgsql-keyword-case
rules.description: Enforce a consistent case (upper or lower) for SQL and PL/pgSQL keywords inside PL/pgSQL function bodies

rules.name: prefer-add-constraint-not-valid
rules.description: Prefer `ADD CONSTRAINT ... NOT VALID` followed by a separate `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` to avoid an `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` lock on the full table while it is being scanned

rules.name: prefer-bigint-id
rules.description: Prefer `bigint` for primary-key `id` columns; `int` / `smallint` primary keys risk silent overflow on growing tables

rules.name: prefer-cast-operator
rules.description: Enforce a single style for type casts (`x::int` operator form vs `CAST(x AS int)` function form)

rules.name: prefer-coalesce-over-case
rules.description: Prefer `COALESCE(x, y)` over `CASE WHEN x IS NULL THEN y ELSE x END` (and its IS NOT NULL mirror)

rules.name: prefer-current-timestamp-over-now
rules.description: Prefer SQL-standard `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` / `CURRENT_TIME` over PostgreSQL's `now()` and the timezone-naive `LOCALTIMESTAMP` / `LOCALTIME`

rules.name: prefer-exists-over-in-subquery
rules.description: Prefer `EXISTS (...)` over `... IN (subquery)` so NULL handling is unambiguous and the planner can use a semi-join

rules.name: prefer-explicit-null-ordering
rules.description: When `ORDER BY` specifies an explicit direction, require an explicit `NULLS FIRST` / `NULLS LAST` so null ordering is not implicit

rules.name: prefer-in-list-over-or
rules.description: Prefer `x IN (a, b, c)` over a chain of `x = a OR x = b OR x = c`

rules.name: prefer-keyword-case
rules.description: Enforce a consistent case (upper or lower) for SQL keywords

rules.name: prefer-not-equals-operator
rules.description: Enforce a single style for the not-equal operator (`<>` or `!=`)

rules.name: require-fk-include-columns
rules.description: Require every foreign-key constraint to include a configured set of columns (e.g. `tenant_id` in a multi-tenant database)

rules.name: require-if-exists
rules.description: Require `IF EXISTS` on every `DROP` statement so re-running a migration on a database that already lost the object does not error

rules.name: require-index-on-fk-column
rules.description: Require an index on every foreign-key column; without it a `DELETE` or `UPDATE` on the referenced table sequentially scans the referencing table to enforce the constraint

rules.name: require-limit
rules.description: Require LIMIT clause in SELECT statements

rules.name: require-named-constraint
rules.description: Require an explicit name on table-level CHECK, UNIQUE, FOREIGN KEY, and EXCLUSION constraints

rules.name: require-on-delete-action
rules.description: Require an explicit `ON DELETE` clause on every foreign-key constraint

rules.name: require-primary-key
rules.description: Require every `CREATE TABLE` to declare a primary key

rules.name: require-schema-qualified-table
rules.description: Require `CREATE TABLE` to use a schema-qualified name (e.g. `audit.events`) so the target schema is explicit

rules.name: require-table-columns
rules.description: Require every `CREATE TABLE` to include a configured set of columns (e.g. multi-tenant / audit columns like `tenant_id`, `created_at`, `updated_by`)

rules.name: require-trailing-semicolon
rules.description: Require a trailing `;` at the end of the SQL file

rules.name: require-where-in-delete
rules.description: Require a WHERE clause in DELETE statements

rules.name: require-where-in-update
rules.description: Require a WHERE clause in UPDATE statements

rules.name: snake-case-column-name
rules.description: Require column names to be snake_case

rules.name: snake-case-table-name
rules.description: Require table names to be snake_case
